Title: Insurance sector faces slowdown, but Tune Protect eyes growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tune Protect Group Bhd expects the insurance industry to remain largely subdued over the next one to two years amid geopolitical uncertainties.
Tune Protect chief strategy officer Koot Chiew Ling, however, said it is confident of outperforming the broader market.
He said the insurer is targeting growth above the industry average by strengthening its general insurance business, expanding travel-related offerings and growing ancillary services.
She said Tune Protect remains cautious about the travel insurance segment due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East.
The company is confident its diversified growth strategy will help offset potential headwinds.
"We expect our growth to be better than the industry.
"While the industry may appear stagnant, we do not see that in ourselves because we have other growth drivers, particularly our general insurance business and ancillary offerings," she told reporters at a media luncheon here today.
Koot said Tune Protect's growth strategy focuses on three areas namely its Malaysian general insurance business, regional travel insurance operations and ancillary products and services.
The focus this year is to grow its general insurance business after returning the segment to profitability last year, with motor insurance expected to be a key contributor.
For its regional business, Koot said Tune Protect remains cautious on travel insurance growth amid geopolitical uncertainties and is expanding its distribution channels through online travel agencies (OTAs) and traditional travel agents.
Meanwhile, she said ancillary products are becoming an increasingly important revenue contributor.
Tune Protect's Delay Lounge Pass recorded six-fold growth and generated about RM20 million in revenue, while the company has expanded related offerings to include electronic SIM (e-SIM) services and baggage trackers.
The company is also introducing ancillary benefits linked to motor insurance, such as complimentary valet parking for selected policyholders.
On industry challenges, Koot said the conflict in the Middle East remains a key concern for the company due to its exposure to the travel insurance market.
She said Tune Protect has already seen some decline in travel-related business from business-to-business channels, although airline-related volumes have yet to be significantly affected.
The company is closely monitoring the situation, particularly the potential impact of higher fuel prices on airlines, which could indirectly affect travel demand and insurance sales.
To mitigate these risks, Koot said Tune Protect is focusing on opportunities in domestic and Asean travel markets, where demand remains resilient.
She added that the company is also pursuing growth in motor insurance through partnerships with automotive franchise networks, digital banks and e-wallet providers.
Meanwhile, Koot said customer response to the group's direct-to-consumer Travel Easy insurance product has remained strong.
She noted Travel Easy as one of Tune Protect's top-performing online products due to its ease of purchase, direct customer rebates and seamless digital claims experience.
"The performance has been good. Customers can purchase and submit claims through the same platform, making the entire journey much more seamless," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Daim's daughter wins bid to recover passport
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today ordered the return of the passport of Asnida Daim, daughter of the late former finance minister Tun Daim Zainuddin, within seven days.
Judge Nurulhuda Nur'aini Mohamad Nor said there was no basis to retain the passport of the 65-year-old, a condition imposed by the Sessions Court alongside RM50,000 bail after she was charged in April.
"The issue of non-compliance with attendance could not be proven. Therefore, to link the non-compliance issue to a risk of flight if the passport is returned to and retained by the applicant (Asnida) is unjustified.
"The court allows this review application. The applicant's passport is to be returned to her within seven days from today," she said.
The ruling followed submissions by Asnida's lawyers, Rajesh Nagarajan and Sachpreetraj Singh, and deputy public prosecutor Nidzuwan Abd Latip.
Rajesh argued that the Sessions Court imposed the passport surrender condition without supporting evidence or justification from the prosecution, in breach of established legal principles and Asnida's constitutional rights.
He submitted that the lower court's order was inconsistent with a binding Court of Appeal decision involving Toh Puan Na'imah Abdul Khalid, in which a similar passport impoundment order was set aside on identical grounds.
Asked by the judge, Rajesh said Asnida travelled frequently for business as her family had business interests overseas, but had remained in the country since April because her passport had been impounded.
"The fact that the applicant travels abroad is not evidence of a flight risk or an intention to abscond.
"For the prosecution's assertion to carry any weight, there must be a history of non-attendance or evasion of process. None exists and none has been presented to the court.
"The prosecution has not demonstrated any compelling reason to justify the continued impoundment of the passport," he said.
Rajesh further argued that, contrary to the prosecution's claim, Asnida had cooperated with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) throughout its investigations and that any delays in attending the MACC office were communicated through her lawyers and subsequently resolved.
He said Asnida submitted statutory declarations declaring her assets on Dec 7, 2023, in response to notices issued under Section 36(1) of the MACC Act dated Nov 8 and Nov 11 that year.
Rajesh also pointed out that Asnida had voluntarily appeared in court to face the charge, arguing that the prosecution's claim that she was uncooperative and posed a flight risk was unfounded.
In reply, Nidzuwan maintained that Asnida posed a flight risk, citing her alleged non-compliance with the MACC investigation after she failed to attend appointments to have her statement recorded in February and March, appearing only on April 6 before being charged the following day.
"She is facing a non-bailable offence, not a minor one. This is a matter of public interest and there is a criminal charge hanging over her.
"If convicted, she faces a sentence. Therefore, there is a risk that she may abscond if given the opportunity," he said.
Nidzuwan argued that Asnida's alleged non-compliance, frequent overseas travel for business and possible residence abroad indicated the capability and tendency to flee.
He submitted that the RM50,000 bail and surrender of her passport were fair and reasonable, and that she could apply for the temporary release of her passport when necessary.
On April 7, Asnida pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court to a charge of failing to declare her assets.
She was accused of intentionally furnishing a written statement on oath that did not comply with the terms of a notice issued under Section 36(1)(b) of the MACC Act, which was served on her on Nov 14, 2023.
Among the assets allegedly not declared were shareholdings in five companies: Amiraz Sdn Bhd, Arida Farms Sdn Bhd, Kangkung Catering Sdn Bhd, Transgrade Sdn Bhd and Gajah3 Foods Sdn Bhd.
The offence falls under Section 36(2) of the Act, which carries a maximum penalty of five years' imprisonment, a fine of up to RM100,000, or both, upon conviction.
MACC had issued notices to trace Daim's four children following their failure to respond to and comply with two official notices regarding asset declarations.
Besides Asnida, Daim's other children are Md Wira Dani, Muhammed Amir Zainuddin and Muhammed Amin Zainuddin.
MACC said the notices were issued after no response was received and attempts to contact all four had failed as of Feb 26.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taiwan fires battle-tested rockets in 'shoot-and-scoot' anti-invasion drill
Article:
TAICHUNG: Taiwan's military on Wednesday fired its new mobile HIMARS rocket system, which is widely used by Ukraine, simulating an attack on an invading Chinese force and demonstrating its ability to "shoot-and-scoot" by avoiding counter strikes.
China, which views democratically governed Taiwan as its own territory, has never renounced the use of force to bring the island under its control, and its warplanes and warships operate almost daily around the island.
Taiwan test fired its Lockheed Martin-made High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems, or HIMARS, for the first time last year off its east coast.
Wednesday marked the first time it has fired the precision weapon on the west coast, in central Taiwan's Taichung.
The military said the drill was to demonstrate the HIMARS' mobility and ability to "shoot-and-scoot" - withdrawing after firing to avoid being locked onto by enemy radar - so "greatly improving battlefield survivability."
"Our HIMARS demonstrated the solid combat capabilities of the unit and successfully completed this training," company commander Ko Ming-pin said.
HIMARS is one of Ukraine's main strike systems and has been used multiple times during the war with Russia.
The beaches and mud flats on Taiwan's west coast, directly facing China across the Taiwan Strait, are seen as the most likely location for an attempted landing by the Chinese military in the event of any invasion.
Taiwan's military is modernising to enable it to fight an asymmetric war with more mobile weapons that can still pack a punch like the HIMARS, to turn the island into a "porcupine" that is hard to attack and can survive a Chinese assault.
With a range of about 300 km, HIMARS could strike coastal targets in China's southeastern province of Fujian on the other side of the Taiwan Strait.
The weapon would be used with Taiwan's domestically developed Thunderbolt-2000 launchers so Chinese forces could be targeted as they left port or attempted to land on Taiwan's coast.
Thunderbolts were fired on the first day of the drill on Tuesday.
Taiwan's government rejects China's sovereignty claims, saying only the island's people can decide their future.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan offers free bus rides to cultural festival, targets 300,000 visitors
Article:
KOTA BARU: Kelantan is offering free bus rides from Kuala Lumpur to its annual cultural festival as the state targets 300,000 visitors, including tourists from Thailand.
State Tourism, Culture, Arts and Heritage Committee chairman Datuk Kamarudin Md Nor said the initiative was aimed at encouraging more visitors, particularly those from outside the state, to attend the Kelantan People's Cultural Festival 2026.
The four-day festival will be held at Tok Bali Jetty in Pasir Puteh from July 1 to 4.
"We have arranged a free bus service from Kuala Lumpur to Tok Bali for visitors who wish to attend the festival.
"The sponsorship by a local company reflects strong support from the private sector in promoting Kelantan's arts, culture and tourism products," he said after launching the festival.
[[nid:1459802]]
Kamarudin said the event would feature a wide range of cultural performances, traditional arts showcases and food offerings throughout the four days.
He said the festival was expected to attract about 300,000 visitors, including residents from Kelantan and neighbouring states.
"We also believe the festival will attract visitors from southern Thailand.
"We are confident the festival will become one of the state's major tourism attractions this year and contribute to the local economy through increased visitor spending," he said.
The Kelantan People's Cultural Festival aims to preserve and promote the state's rich cultural heritage while providing a platform for local artistes, performers and entrepreneurs to showcase their talents and products.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Alliance Bank commits RM2bil to boost SME financing access
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd has committed up to RM2 billion in financing solutions for SMEs through a partnership with Credit Guarantee Corp Malaysia Bhd (CGC).
The financing will be supported by CGC's portfolio guarantee, allowing eligible SMEs to access funds for working capital and business expansion.
Under the arrangement, Alliance Bank will be the financing provider, while CGC will provide guarantee support to help businesses with limited collateral access funding.
Alliance Bank group chief executive officer Kellee Kam said the initiative reflects the bank's commitment to supporting Malaysian SMEs.
"We recognise that collateral requirements can stifle viable businesses.
"Through this portfolio guarantee, we are dismantling those barriers to ensure entrepreneurs have the liquidity they need to innovate, scale and navigate economic headwinds with confidence."
CGC president and chief executive officer Mohamed Nazri Omar said the collaboration would strengthen SME access to financing and support businesses across different stages of growth.
"The RM2 billion portfolio guarantee will enable more businesses to obtain the funding they need for working capital and expansion, while enhancing their resilience amid an evolving economic landscape," he said.
Alliance Bank said the initiative comes as SMEs face a more challenging operating environment, with timely financing needed to maintain operations, accelerate digitalisation and strengthen market competitiveness.
The bank has also streamlined its application process to improve turnaround times while maintaining prudent risk management practices.
Since 2018, Alliance Bank has disbursed close to RM3 billion under various CGC guarantee schemes, benefiting more than 5,000 SMEs nationwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MyDigital ID's high-security features can help curb online fraud — CCID director
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The MyDigital ID system, equipped with high-security features, has the potential to curb fraud while strengthening identity verification, says Bukit Aman.
Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said police held two engagement sessions with MyDigital ID and were confident its full deployment could help prevent online scams.
"We believe that when MyDigital ID is fully utilised, it will be one of the mechanisms to help prevent online fraud," he said at the Bank Heroes #JanganKenaScam 2026 Appreciation Ceremony in Taman Tun Dr Ismail today.
On concerns over data leaks, Rusdi said the system was equipped with multiple security layers designed to reduce the risk of identity fraud.
"We are working to ensure MyDigital ID is used in transactions across all banks," he said.
National Taskforce to Combat Fraud chairman Syed Ahmad Taufik Albar said integration of MyDigital ID with banks' Know Your Customer (KYC) systems would strengthen anti-fraud efforts.
However, he said there was no fixed timeline for implementation as each bank would adopt the technology at its own pace.
Earlier, 68 bank officers were recognised for detecting and stopping fraud attempts, collectively saving RM139 million in customer funds over the past year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bullying cases drop at MRSMs with former military wardens
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Several Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) schools have recorded fewer disciplinary and bullying cases since former military personnel were appointed as full-time wardens.
Majlis Amanah Rakyat chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said a study by Universiti Putra Malaysia researchers found a significant decline in cases at MRSMs that had previously recorded such incidents.
"Mara's approach of placing former military personnel at MRSM has proven effective in addressing bullying.
"In fact, student discipline has improved significantly while also reducing the burden on teachers who previously had to take on additional responsibilities as hostel wardens," he said in a Facebook post today.
On April 8, 2025, Asyraf had proposed that
retired police and military personnel
be appointed as full-time wardens to help curb bullying in MRSM hostels, following several viral incidents of student misconduct.
All
58 MRSM schools
would have full-time wardens from among former uniformed personnel beginning next year, selected through a strict screening process.
Asyraf said the selected candidates would also undergo specialised training to familiarise themselves with Mara's education philosophy.
He added that the initiative is being rolled out in phases, with 18 MRSMs implementing the warden system from June before full nationwide expansion next year.
Last year, MRSM had also launched a six-month pilot project placing former military personnel in student hostels to address bullying and violence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah's Gaya Island cleanliness push aims to attract more tourists
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Kota Kinabalu City Hall (DBKK) plans to strengthen cleanliness efforts on Gaya Island to attract more tourists.
Mayor Datuk Seri Sabin Samitah said the island's natural beauty could attract more visitors if cleanliness was improved.
"If we look around, this island is beautiful. I hope that through programmes like this, we can further improve the cleanliness here.
"Tourists who come to Malaysia and Sabah want to visit places like this — places with nature and village homes — but they are also very concerned about cleanliness.
"If cleanliness is not maintained, they may be afraid to visit because they worry about diseases such as dengue. That is why we also have health awareness programmes," he told reporters after the LA21 i-Fit community programme at Kampung Torong-Logong here.
He added that DBKK wanted the local community's involvement in looking after the island, which is about a 10-minute boat ride from the mainland.
The local authority would provide more rubbish bins and increase waste collection efforts, including using small boats to collect rubbish from the sea, as well as hiring local residents to assist with rubbish collection and disposal.
"That way, we would not need to bring in workers from elsewhere. We will look into the payment mechanism later so residents can help collect and dispose of rubbish at designated areas," he said.
On squatter management, Sabin said City Hall had structured enforcement plans involving regular operations against illegal settlements on the island.
He said authorities would first conduct a census in squatter areas and assess relocation options for local residents if necessary.
"However, if they are illegal immigrants who came here unlawfully, we will inform them that their settlements will be demolished."
To date, DBKK has demolished 986 houses.
Earlier, Sabin took part in Zumba, coconut planting and visits to exhibition booths during the programme.
He said such programmes helped authorities better understand the challenges faced by island communities, including the need for improved public facilities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former CFO of condom maker Karex charged with insider trading
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A former chief financial officer of condom manufacturer Karex Bhd was charged in the Sessions Court today with insider trading involving the company's shares six years ago.
Goh Chok Siang, 54, was accused of acquiring 150,000 units of Karex shares on July 22, 2020, while in possession of material non-public information regarding the company's intention to venture into the manufacturing and sale of gloves.
He pleaded not guilty when the charge was read to him before judge Izralizam Sanusi.
The offence under Section 188(2)(a) of the Capital Markets and Services Act 2007 carries a maximum 10-year jail term and a fine of not less than RM1 million upon conviction.
The court allowed bail at RM90,000 with two local sureties.
Goh was also ordered to surrender his passport and report to the Securities Commission's investigating officer monthly until the trial concludes.
Deputy public prosecutors Ng Chian Huey and Izzat Fauzan, together with prosecuting officer Mark Rohan Mahadevan, prosecuted, while Wong Chiang Kiat appeared for the accused.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perak closes hiking trail for up to 6 months after missing hiker incident
Article:
KUANTAN: A hiking trail in Perak will be closed for up to six months while authorities review safety procedures following a case in which a woman hiker went missing for 14 days before being found safe.
The Perak Forestry Department is reviewing standard operating procedures (SOPs) for future hiking activities at Gunung Batu Putih.
Its director Datuk Mohd Basri Abdul Manaf said the hiking trail within the Bukit Tapah Forest Reserve will be closed for between three and six months.
"We need to review and improve several SOPs to ensure compliance among Forestry Mountain Guides (FMG) and hikers. Cooperation between the department, FMG members and the Orang Asli community must also be strengthened for the benefit of all parties.
"We do not want visitors entering Perak's forest reserves for recreational activities, including mountain climbing, to be exposed to unwanted incidents," he said when met at Yayasan Pahang here today.
The move follows the incident involving 49-year-old hiker
Jaslinda Saludin
, who went missing before being found safe on June 6.
Basri was speaking on the sidelines of the 46th Peninsular Malaysia Forestry Department Directors' Conference.
He said the site would only be reopened after SOP reviews are completed and consultations are held with relevant agencies.
Meanwhile, Perak FMG deputy head Nur Shahira Abdul Rauf said the association welcomed the temporary closure to allow improvements to safety procedures.
She said Gunung Batu Putih was a popular hiking destination, with activities taking place almost every week until the recent incident involving Jaslinda Saludin.
"FMG members will now bring hikers to other mountains and hiking destinations in Perak, including five major peaks that remain popular among visitors," she said, adding that FMG carried out
trail-clearing and route-marking works
at Gunung Batu Putih last year.
Jaslinda was among 14 hikers and two mountain guides who began the Trans Spencer Chapman expedition on May 23 via the Pos Gedong–Gunung Bah Gading–Gunung Batu Putih–Kuala Woh route.
She was reported missing the following day, triggering a large-scale search and rescue operation before being found safe on June 6.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Packaging rebound lifts Scientex's Q3 earnings to RM142.2mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Scientex Bhd posted a 14.8 per cent increase in net profit for the third quarter ended April 30, 2026 (Q3 FY26) to RM142.2 million from RM123.9 million a year earlier.
This was driven primarily by strong recovery in its packaging division, according to Scientex.
Its revenue for the quarter edged up 0.7 per cent year-on-year to RM1.12 billion.
The packaging division recorded revenue of RM630 million, up 2.5 per cent from RM614.8 million previously, supported by higher sales volume.
Scientex's operating profit more than doubled to RM73.2 million from RM34.5 million, benefiting from improved market sentiment, stronger margins and a more optimised product mix.
The company's property division maintained steady performance, generating revenue of RM487.8 million compared with RM495.8 million a year ago.
The division's operating profit eased to RM129.5 million from RM143 million, with the slight decline in revenue attributed to the timing of several project launches scheduled for the coming quarter.
Scientex declared an interim dividend of six sen per share for FY26, amounting to a total payout of RM93.4 million.
The shares will trade ex-dividend on June 29 with payment scheduled for July 17.
Scientex chief executive officer Lim Peng Jin said it remains focused on strengthening its packaging operations amid global market uncertainties while continuing to expand its affordable housing footprint.
On the property front, Lim said healthy take-up rates across new launches continue to support the group's township expansion plans.
Scientex has delivered more than 45,000 homes to date, surpassing 90 per cent of its Vision 2028 target of 50,000 homes nationwide.
For the nine months ended April 30, 2026, Scientex reported an increase in net profit by 11.6 per cent to RM420.3 million from RM376.4 million.
Its revenue increased by 2.5 per cent to RM 3.41 billion from RM3.33 billion in the corresponding period last year.
Scientex said its packaging division will continue prioritising cost optimisation and operational efficiency to enhance resilience and competitiveness amid uncertain market conditions.
The property division is also expanding its development pipeline with three new township projects planned in Melaka, Kedah and Selangor.
The projects comprise Scientex Melaka in Cheng, Scientex SP Astana in Sungai Petani and Scientex Bestari Jaya, which are expected to support the group's medium- to long-term growth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DAP to retain over half of incumbents for Negri election
Article:
SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan DAP will retain more than half of its incumbent representatives as candidates in the upcoming state election as it seeks to maintain its impressive record of winning every seat it has contested since 2013.
DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said the party's candidate list is now in its final stages and an announcement will be made within the next few days.
He said the party also intends to field several new faces in the election as it seeks to defend the 11 seats it won in the last state election.
"The candidate list will be announced early to give them time to make preparations and familiarise themselves with the constituencies they will contest, particularly the new candidates.
"Any elected representative who is replaced must assist and support the new candidate to ensure victory and maintain the party's strength," he said to reporters after launching the Lobak Electronic Screen at Dataran Lobak here today.
It was previously reported that Loke, who is also state DAP chairman, said that he would defend the Chennah seat in the upcoming election.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced the dissolution of the Negri Sembilan state legislative assembly effective June 5 to pave the way for the state election, which must be held within 60 days.
The 2023 Negri Sembilan election saw Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) form the state government after securing 31 of the 36 seats.
PH won 17 seats while BN secured 14 seats, with Perikatan Nasional winning five seats.
Negri Sembilan became the second state to dissolve its legislative assembly after Johor did so on June 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MyDigital ID integration could boost fraud detection
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The full integration of MyDigital ID into banks' know your customer systems could strengthen the banking sector's verification capabilities, making it a valuable tool in combating scams, industry executives said.
The national taskforce to combat fraud chairman Syed Ahmad Taufik Albar said there is currently no specific timeline for the full integration of MyDigital ID.
The initiative remains a work in progress and has already commenced,  he added.
"Each bank has a different starting point. They all have their respective timelines in terms of adopting MyDigital ID and exploring more use cases on how to utilise MyDigital ID to prevent scams," said Syed Ahmad, who is also Malayan Banking Bhd group chief executive officer of community financial services.
Police commissioner and director of the Commercial Crime Investigation Department Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa believes that once MyDigital ID is fully implemented, it will serve as an important tool in helping to prevent scams.
"It is only a matter of time, we are confident it will be widely adopted across the banking sector.
"MyDigital ID comes with multiple security features that we strongly believe are robust and difficult to compromise. Most importantly, these safeguards are powerful enough to help prevent scams," he said.
Syed and Rusdi made the remarks at a press conference following the #JanganKenaScam Bank Heroes Recognition 2026.
Meanwhile, Syed said there has been an increase in the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to carry out scams, but banks worldwide are also investing in strengthening their capabilities by leveraging AI to detect and prevent fraud.
"The use of AI is becoming more pervasive in all aspects of our lives. In the area of scamming, it is no exception, so there is a rise in the use of AI capabilities to commit scams.
"There is a continuous investment that needs to happen from now going forward," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: How Rosmah's RM67.5mil jewellery case unfolded: A timeline
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Global Royalty claimed that it sent 44 pieces of jewellery including diamond necklaces, earrings, rings, bracelets and tiaras, each worth US$124,000 (RM519,183) to US$925,000 (RM3.8 million) to Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor through hand-carry courier via its two agents.
Today, the High Court ordered Rosmah to pay RM67.5 million to the jeweller after
finding her liable
for failing to return 43 pieces of jewellery entrusted to her on a consignment basis.
Here's a timeline of events:
May 17, 2018 - Police from Bukit Aman's Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID), led by Datuk Seri Amar Singh Ishar Singh, conducted a major raid at Pavilion Residences in Kuala Lumpur.
June 26, 2018 - Global Royalty filed the lawsuit against Rosmah. The Beirut-based firm said it had on Feb 10, sent the 44 pieces of jewellery, which included diamond necklaces, earrings, rings, bracelets and a tiara, each costing between US$124,000 and US$925,000.
The company claimed that Rosmah, in a letter dated May 22, acknowledged receiving those items.
Feb 14, 2019 - The High Court ordered Rosmah to check and confirm in seven days the existence of the 44 pieces of jewellery that were in her possession before the seizure by police kept at the Bank Negara Malaysia's vault.
May 24, 2019 – The company confirmed that it had inspected 12,000 pieces of jewellery seized by police in a series of raids shortly after the 14th general election.
Sept 17, 2019 - The High Court ordered a Lebanese jeweller to deposit RM75,000 as security for costs in the suit by Oct 11.
May 29, 2024 – High Court dismissed Rosmah's application to include the Inspector-General of Police (IGP) and the government as the third party in this suit.
June 7, 2024 – Rosmah applied for a stay of the suit pending the decision of her appeal at the Court of Appeal against a High Court decision which dismissed her application to include the government and the police as the third party in the case.
March 27, 2025 – A unanimous three-judge panel, consisting of Justices Datuk P. Ravinthran, Datuk Dr Choo Kah Sing, and Datuk Seri Mohd Firuz Jaffril allowed Rosmah's appeal to include the IGP and the government as third party in this suit.
April 13, 2026
- The hearing for the main lawsuit took place over three days, from April 13 to April 15, 2026. Both Samer Hassib Halimeh (managing director of Global Royalty Trading) and Rosmah took the stand to testify.
June 10, 2026 – Rosmah ordered to pay RM67.5 million to Global Royalty after the High Court found her liable for failing to return 43 pieces of jewellery entrusted to her on a consignment basis.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 4 remanded for 7 days over alleged Perkeso false claims
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Four suspects, including a woman, have been remanded for seven days over alleged false claims involving Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) Daya Kerjaya employment incentives.
Magistrate Arun Noval Dass allowed the remand application against the suspects, aged between 40 and 60, until Tuesday to facilitate investigations under Section 18 of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act.
According to reports, the suspects allegedly posed as workers of a contracting company and submitted claims totalling between RM160,000 and RM180,000.
MACC investigations found the suspects were not actual employees of the company and are believed to have cheated to obtain the incentives.
Perkeso's Daya Kerjaya initiative provides training and job placement assistance to help unemployed individuals improve their skills and secure employment.
Under Perkeso conditions, eligible recipients include senior citizens aged 60 and above, persons with disabilities (OKU), and individuals who have worked at a company for at least six months.
Suspicion over the claims led to the MACC probe and the subsequent arrest of the suspects.
Yesterday, MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the commission had detected misappropriation involving the government initiative, with
false claims
amounting to RM9.01 million.
He said investigations identified 143 companies involving 320 workers nationwide implicated in the misappropriation of government funds managed by Perkeso since 2024.
Under the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 programme, the government provides employers with incentives in the form of RM1,500 monthly salary support for six months for each hired worker from target groups, including persons with disabilities, former convicts and senior citizens.
However, Abd Halim said initial intelligence revealed several implementation issues.
"Among them, we found individuals declared as employees who did not actually exist.
"MACC also detected workers who did not meet the minimum six-month employment requirement but received full payments, as well as the use of forged documents to obtain the
incentive payments
," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia child marriage: Suhakam pushes to raise Muslim legal marriage age to 18
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) is advocating a nationwide minimum marriage age of 18 for Muslims, while retaining a judicial mechanism that would allow minors aged 16 and 17 to apply for permission to marry.
Suhakam Chief Children's Commissioner Dr Farah Nini Dusuki said the proposed reform was aimed at safeguarding children's rights, education and health, while ensuring marriage remained an option only in exceptional cases involving minors.
"We are not restricting the age of marriage.
"We are just saying that when we raise the age to 18 and if you want to get married when you're 16 or 17, you'll have to go to court," she said.
Speaking at the Legal Symposium on Children's Rights to Access to Justice and Effective Remedies today, Farah said underage marriages were often used to conceal pre-marital pregnancies, although marriage was not necessarily the best solution.
She said the courts should play a central role in determining whether a marriage involving a minor was in the child's best interests.
"The duty of the court is to ensure the best interest of the child. For example, why enter into marriage just to cover up a pregnancy?
"So when you go to the court, then the court will be obliged to look at your medical report, your social report, the police report, the criminal record of your prospective partner.
"We want to safeguard the children's health, education, basically the right to be a child," she said.
[[nid:1459757]]
\
She noted that Suhakam recently hosted a national Muzakarah (expert discussion) with religious leaders to gather feedback and advocate for child protection by raising the minimum marriage age—subject to judicial exceptions—a proposal to which the religious authorities agreed in principle.
"Next Monday we will be going to Sibu for a child marriage convention to speak to over 300 indigenous community leaders.
"It's still work in progress, but we are there to listen to them to understand the situation of child marriage in Sarawak better," she said.
Currently, Selangor and Kedah are the only Malaysian states to have increased the legal minimum age of marriage to 18 where those under those ages, will have to apply to court to get married.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: University students across South Korea to protest in ballot shortage row
Article:
SEOUL: University students across South Korea will stage protests on Wednesday as anger grew over ballot paper shortages at recent local elections.
Dozens of polling stations nationwide were short of around 7,000 ballot papers on June 3 election day, official data showed.
The supplies were eventually replenished on voting day, but the mishap fuelled fury in a nation where unfounded claims of vote tampering have found a growing audience.
The local election was the first nationwide vote since President Lee Jae Myung took office following conservative Yoon Suk Yeol's ouster over his brief martial law declaration in late 2024.
Lee's ruling liberal Democratic Party won most races in the vote for mayors, local government officials and assembly members, but failed to flip the critical Seoul mayoral seat.
The head of the election watchdog has resigned over the ballot paper row, but authorities have not offered to hold a new vote, sparking huge protests at the weekend.
Student unions at 18 universities said they will hold new demonstrations from 6 pm Wednesday.
"We are determined to protest because people are universally outraged regardless of political affiliation," Hwang In-seo of Yonsei University's student council, told AFP.
Analysts say the National Election Commission, a constitutional body with limited external oversight, has long faced gaps in internal discipline and review mechanisms.
A Seoul court this week ordered evidence from an affected polling station in the capital be preserved for investigation, while local media reported prosecutors and police had agreed to set up a joint investigation team.
The evidence includes ballot boxes and CCTV footage, a representative of the Seoul Eastern District Court told AFP.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Warisan remains strong despite defections, says Shafie
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Parti Warisan remains strong despite the recent departure of several former leaders, its president Datuk Seri Mohd Shafie Apdal said.
Shafie said those who left the party were merely a handful of former division chiefs and former office-bearers.
He said he had taken note of the decision by several individuals to leave the party recently.
However, he said that none of them were elected representatives chosen by the people.
"What has happened is merely a small chapter in a much longer journey.
"In politics, every party goes through different phases. Those who are truly sincere and committed to the struggle will remain.
"Warisan remains intact and will continue to move forward. The struggle for Sabah will remain our foremost priority," he said in a statement today.
The former chief minister was referring to several leaders, including former Warisan Supreme Council member and Kota Belud Warisan vice-chief Taib Isai, who recently joined Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor's Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah.
Taib was reported as saying that the decision was driven by growing dissatisfaction among grassroots members and concerns that the party had strayed from Sabah's aspirations.
Shafie said Warisan's strength had never depended on any individual.
"Our strength lies in ordinary members, grassroots volunteers and the people of Sabah who continue to believe in a clean and principled struggle for this state.
"A political movement becomes stronger when those who are truly committed remain, while those whose loyalty depends on positions, candidacies or personal interests choose to leave."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pensioner remanded in Johor after granddaughter alleges repeated sexual abuse
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A 67-year-old pensioner has been remanded for six days after his teenage granddaughter alleged repeated sexual abuse over the past year, police said.
Magistrate Arun Noval Dass granted the remand order to allow police to complete investigations under Section 376(3) of the Penal Code.
Police received a report two days ago after the victim confided in her mother. The suspect was subsequently arrested.
Initial investigations found the 13-year-old had been living with her grandfather in Batu Pahat following her parents' divorce.
She was later taken by her mother to a northern state in April.
Although she initially remained silent, the victim later disclosed the alleged abuse to her mother, police said.
Investigations indicate the alleged offences took place throughout last year, with the last incident in December.
Section 376(3) of the Penal Code provides for mandatory imprisonment and whipping upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rotary Club Bangsar taps media partnerships to amplify aid for B40, Orang Asli communities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Rotary Club Bangsar is seeking closer collaboration with media organisations to raise public awareness of its humanitarian and community outreach efforts.
Its president, Ravinder Singh, said the media plays a vital role in highlighting the work carried out by non-governmental organisations (NGOs), particularly initiatives aimed at assisting underserved communities.
"We are looking at how the media can help us let people know what we are doing," he said during a visit by the club's delegation to Balai Berita Bangsar today.
Ravinder said Rotary Club Bangsar regularly organises charitable and outreach programmes, including medical aid and welfare initiatives for Orang Asli communities and the B40 group.
[[nid:1459736]]
He said the visit also provided members with valuable insights into the processes behind news gathering, production and content creation.
"It was an eye-opener. Not many people know what actually happens behind the scenes and how much work goes into producing content," he said.
During the visit, the delegation toured Media Prima Audio, the TV3 and NTV7 studios, the WHI studio, Rev Media Group and the News and Current Affairs Editorial Floor.
Also part of the delegation was Media Prima board member Abdullah Abu Samah.
At the Editorial Floor, the delegation was received by Media Prima News and Current Affairs deputy managing editor and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Abd Karim, NST deputy group editor Sharanjit Singh and Berita Harian deputy group editor M. Thillinadan.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rosmah faces bankruptcy risk if RM67.5mil judgment remains unpaid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Lebanese jeweller Global Royalty Trading SAL Ltd will pursue all legal avenues to recover the RM67.5 million awarded by the High Court against Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor, including bankruptcy proceedings as a last resort.
Senior lawyer Datuk David Gurupatham, who represented the company, said his client intended to fully enforce the judgment delivered today.
"We will execute the judgment until we recover every penny, or she is declared bankrupt. Those are my instructions from the client," he told the New Straits Times.
However, David said bankruptcy proceedings would not be the company's immediate course of action if Rosmah fails to satisfy the judgment within the one-month period ordered by the court.
"We will pursue bankruptcy only if all attempts to enforce the judgment and recover the debt fail," he said.
Earlier, Judge Datuk Quay Chew Soon ordered Rosmah to pay RM67,461,027.37 within one month after finding her liable for failing to return 43 pieces of luxury jewellery supplied on a consignment basis.
Under Malaysian law, a judgment creditor may first seek enforcement through measures such as seizure and sale proceedings, garnishee actions and judgment debtor examinations before resorting to bankruptcy proceedings.
The court found that the jewellery had been delivered to Rosmah for viewing under a consignment arrangement, that she had not paid for the items, and that she failed to return them.
It also dismissed Rosmah's third-party claim against the Inspector-General of Police and the government, rejecting her contention that the missing jewellery had been seized during police raids linked to the 1MDB investigations in 2018.
Meanwhile, Rosmah's lawyer Rajivan Nambiar said the defence would apply for a stay of execution and appeal the decision to the Court of Appeal.
The suit centred on 44 pieces of luxury jewellery worth US$14.57 million (about RM68.3 million), which Global Royalty said were supplied to Rosmah in 2018 on a consignment basis for viewing.
The company claimed the jewellery remained its property and was never returned, prompting the suit for compensation.
Only one item — a diamond emerald bracelet — was recovered and returned to the jeweller following forfeiture proceedings, leaving 43 pieces unaccounted for.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: OGX unit bags RM96mil data centre supply contract
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: OGX Group Bhd has secured a RM96.6 million contract to supply critical infrastructure products for a data centre project.
The internet technology (IT) infrastructure solutions specialist said its subsidiary, OGX Networks Sdn Bhd has received a letter of award (LOA) for the supply of fibre optic cables, patch panels and related materials for data centre applications.
Executive director and managing director Tan Suan Loong said this reflects the confidence placed in OGX Group as a reliable partner in supplying data centre products.
Tan noted that the LOA is expected to strengthen the company's presence in the data centre market and further enhance its participation in the data centre supply chain.
"We remain committed to delivering high quality products that meet the stringent requirements of such projects.
"We believe that this LOA positions us well to capture further opportunities within the data centre ecosystem while contributing to the continued growth of digital infrastructure in Malaysia," he said in a statement.
Under the contract, OGX Group said the products are scheduled for delivery within six months from the date of the award.
"The award comes amid accelerating data centre development across the region, which continues to drive demand for reliable, high-performance infrastructure to support large-scale computing environments," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank staff prevent over RM139mil in fraud attempts last year
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 68 bank employees have helped prevent more than RM139 million in fraudulent transactions in 2025 by detecting suspicious activities and intervening in potential scam cases.
Their efforts contributed to a 46 per cent increase in the value of attempted fraudulent transactions successfully prevented in 2025 compared with 2024.
The employees were recognised at the #JanganKenaScam Bank Heroes Recognition 2026, an awareness programme introduced in 2023 by the Association of Banks in Malaysia (ABM) and the Association of Islamic Banking and Financial Institutions Malaysia (AIBIM).
The programme is also in collaboration with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM).
The national taskforce to combat fraud chairman Syed Ahmad Taufik Albar said the total value of the prevented fraudulent transactions rose to RM1.19 billion in 2025.
"This represents a three-fold increase year-on-year and reflects the industry's continued commitment to strengthening fraud prevention amid an increasingly sophisticated scam landscape," he said.
Syed Ahmad, who also serves as group chief executive officer of community financial services at Malayan Banking Bhd, said the employees' vigilance and dedication have played a role in identifying suspicious activities and stopping fraudulent transactions before additional losses could be incurred.
Meanwhile, police commissioner and director of the commercial crime investigation department Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said the strong collaboration and exchange of information between banks and law enforcement agencies have enhanced efforts to safeguard the public against increasingly sophisticated scam threats.
"PDRM values the strong collaboration and continued support from the banking industry in combating commercial crimes and scams," he said.
ABM and AIBIM said banks and financial institutions have made progress through the implementation of anti-scam measures and security enhancements.
This includes money vault or money lock features, malware protection capabilities, kill switch functions and online self-service security check facilities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Military court jails Indonesian soldiers over acid attack on activist
Article:
JAKARTA: A military court in Indonesia on Wednesday sentenced four soldiers to up to three years in prison for an acid attack on an activist critical of perceived militarisation of the government.
The four, all members of the military's Strategic Intelligence Agency (BAIS), were found guilty of premeditated assault for the attack in March that left Andrie Yunus, 27, blind in one eye.
They were arrested shortly after the attack and BAIS head Yudi Abrimantyo stepped down in what officials said was a "form of responsibility".
But critics say the real authors of the crime have not been identified and likely never will be.
Soldiers Edi Sudarko, 45; Budi Hariyanto Widhi Cahyono, 43; Nandala Dwi Prasetia, 40; and Sami Lakka, 41, engaged in "arrogant conduct", presiding judge Fredy Ferdian Isnartanto said.
[[nid:1459718]]
Military prosecutors contend the soldiers did not act on orders but on their own initiative.
They were said to have been angered by Andrie and another activist interrupting a lawmaker's meeting in 2025 to protest a revision to Indonesia's armed forces law.
The amendment, passed by parliament last year, made it possible for active-duty military personnel to work in 14 government ministries and state institutions — up from 10 before.
Andrie and others had asked for the trial to be held in a civilian court, not a military one, for fear of a cover-up in a country where attacks on activists are rarely punished.
He refused to attend any of the trial hearings, citing health reasons and distrust in the court.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan ranks second in fuel smuggling cases nationwide under Ops Tiris 4.0
Article:
KOTA BARU: Kelantan recorded the second-highest number of fuel smuggling cases nationwide under Ops Tiris 4.0 (Integrated), with 57 cases detected between March 16 and June 3 this year.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said only Sabah recorded a higher number of cases, with 58, while Kedah ranked third with 15 cases.
He said the figures formed part of the overall success of Ops Tiris 4.0, which uncovered 740 cases nationwide, resulting in seizures worth RM30.33 million and the arrest of 253 individuals.
"Highly subsidised commodities such as diesel and RON95 petrol remain the primary targets of smugglers, alongside other controlled goods including cooking oil and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG).
"Sabah's geographical location, with its extensive coastline and border areas, makes it particularly vulnerable to smuggling activities," he said.
Azman said attempts to smuggle controlled goods across Malaysia's borders were being met with intensified enforcement following the launch of Ops Tiris 4.0 in March.
Despite challenges posed by the global energy crisis, he said stricter enforcement had led to a significant increase in detections and enforcement actions.
"The close cooperation between the ministry and enforcement agencies such as the police and the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency has proven effective in closing loopholes exploited by smugglers along the border areas.
"The statistics up to June 3 clearly demonstrate the operation's impact," he said.
Investigations revealed that smugglers continued to adopt various tactics to evade detection, including modifying vehicle fuel tanks to increase storage capacity, using transit premises to store fuel before moving it across borders, falsifying purchase documents and deploying multiple vehicles to make repeated border crossings while carrying small quantities of fuel.
Meanwhile, the New Straits Times found Malaysian subsidised petrol being sold openly in southern Thailand during a survey along the Malaysia-Thailand border from Sungai Golok to Narathiwat.
Bottles and small plastic containers filled with petrol were displayed at makeshift roadside stalls and wooden racks, suggesting that cross-border leakage of subsidised fuel remains a persistent problem despite heightened enforcement.
The wide price gap between fuel in Malaysia and Thailand continues to be a major incentive for smugglers.
Subsidised RON95 petrol is sold in Malaysia at RM2.05 per litre, compared with about 44 baht (RM5.70) per litre in Thailand, making Malaysian fuel significantly cheaper and highly attractive for illegal cross-border trade.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump looms large over Brazil's presidential election
Article:
US President Donald Trump's sometimes bombastic stance towards Brazil has placed him at the centre of debate ahead of the country's tightly fought presidential elections in October.
Given Trump's track record of throwing his weight behind his Latin American allies – like Argentine leader Javier Milei, Colombia's presidential hopeful Abelardo de la Espriella and Nasry Asfura in Honduras, to name but a few – a similar intervention in Brazil appears likely.
"It can be expected that Brazil will also be the target of a US attempt to influence the elections" given Trump's past "partisan interventionism," according to Oliver Stuenkel, a professor at the Getulio Vargas Foundation.
The US leader's influence could be less clear-cut this time around, however.
While celebrating his "excellent chemistry" with leftist President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva, who is seeking reelection, Trump has also called candidate Flavio Bolsonaro, the son of former far-right president Jair Bolsonaro, a "smart young man who loves his country."
Both have visited the White House in recent weeks.
Lula, who has accused Trump of behaving like an "emperor," praised their solid rapport after the pair's meeting in May.
But within days of Bolsonaro's visit the same month, Trump endorsed a cause celebre of Brazil's political right by designating the country's two largest drug cartels as terrorist organisations.
He also announced possible tariff hikes on Brazilian products, much to the displeasure of the Lula administration.
With polls currently projecting a tight second round between Lula and Bolsonaro, the right-wing camp is adamant that victory requires a healthy relationship with Washington.
Bolsonaro took to social media to celebrate his own role in getting Trump to designate the so-called Red Command (CV) and First Capital Command (PCC) crime groups as terrorist organisations.
"On a trip as a pre-candidate, we did more for Brazil and the safety of Brazilians than the Workers' Party and Lula did in 17 years in office," he boasted.
Tens of millions of Brazilians living in working-class areas are highly concerned by organised crime and the violence it generates.
[[nid:1459713]]
"The US measure benefits Flavio and wears down Lula, who has always been against it," pro-Bolsonaro lawmaker Sostenes Cavalcante told AFP.
Cavalcante views the American leader as a "decisive factor" in the campaign, but said the Trump card must be used prudently.
"We must be very careful, because he has a positive side that benefits us but also a high disapproval rating," he said, alluding to polls showing that Brazilians are divided over the US president.
Despite enormous ideological differences, Lula has managed to keep Trump on his side – but only sometimes.
Washington's decision to slap sky-high tariffs on Brazil in 2025 as retaliation for convicting Jair Bolsonaro of plotting a coup marked a sour patch in the pair's relations.
Most of those tariffs have since disappeared, but the recent proposal to impose 25 per cent tariffs on some Brazilian products for alleged unfair trade practices has once again rankled Brasilia.
Lula blamed the increased tariffs on Bolsonaro's whisperings in Washington, calling the right-wing candidate a "traitor" to his own export-reliant country.
Although Bolsonaro denies the allegation, "the tariffs cancelled out the advantage Flavio had gained from the US decision regarding the PCC and the CV," said Stuenkel.
Lula is attempting to negotiate directly with Trump but says he is being blocked by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, a Cuban-American whom the leftist has called a "mortal enemy" of Latin America.
Nevertheless, "the Trump-Lula channel is the best thing" the US-Brazil relationship currently has, Bruna Santos, Brazil director at the Inter-American Dialogue think tank, told AFP.
"Their last meeting is what calmed tempers," she said.
Both leaders have confirmed their attendance at the G7 summit in France from June 15 to 17, but it remains unknown if they will meet one-on-one during the event.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Annuar Musa appointed Johor Pas election director for state polls
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pas central committee member Tan Sri Annuar Musa has been appointed Johor Pas election director for the 16th state election.
Johor Pas information chief Mohd Firdaus Jaffar said the appointment was aimed at strengthening the party's machinery ahead of the state polls, which are expected to be held soon.
"Johor Pas welcomes the appointment of Tan Sri Annuar Musa as election director and is confident his experience and leadership will strengthen preparations and coordination of the party machinery for the Johor election.
"As polling day approaches, all Johor Pas machinery are urged to strengthen unity, intensify grassroots efforts and focus on election preparations," he said in a statement today.
Meanwhile, Mohd Firdaus said Johor Pas fully supported the party leadership's decision to end political cooperation with Bersatu.
He said the matter was discussed at a special meeting of the Johor Pas liaison committee yesterday, where members unanimously agreed to respect the decision.
"Johor Pas believes the move does not undermine efforts towards ummah unity, which remains central to the party's struggle.
"We will continue to support any political understanding or electoral pact decided by the central leadership for the upcoming election," he said.
The Johor legislative assembly was dissolved on June 1 with the consent of the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, giving the Election Commission 60 days to hold the state election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mahfuz: Pas-Bersatu 'mock divorce' insults voters
Article:
ALOR SETAR: Amanah described the ongoing political rift between Pas and Bersatu within PN as "political theatre" and a "mock divorce" driven by power struggles rather than principles.
Amanah vice-president Datuk Mahfuz Omar said the latest move by the Pas leadership to end political cooperation with Bersatu while
retaining
its leadership position within PN was contradictory and an insult to the voters' intelligence.
He said the situation was akin to a "divorce on paper but still living under one roof", with political actors continuing to share the same platform while competing for control, influence and positions within the coalition.
"This is nothing more than political drama. They talk about separation, but still cling to leadership structures. The rakyat is being treated like spectators in a scripted performance," he said in a statement today.
Mahfuz said the internal developments showed that political survival was being prioritised over principle.
He argued that if Pas truly believed Bersatu was no longer relevant to its political direction, it should leave PN entirely rather than maintain control within the coalition's structure.
He also accused Pas of using ideological and religious rhetoric to justify internal manoeuvring while grassroots supporters were left to defend decisions made by the party leadership.
"This is politics of convenience. When it suits them, they talk about principles. When it does not, they talk about strategy," he said.
Mahfuz further claimed that the ongoing tensions reflected an attempt to shift responsibility within the coalition by forcing political opponents to initiate formal disengagement rather than taking decisive action themselves.
He said such internal disputes were out of touch with public concerns, noting that Malaysians were grappling with rising living costs, economic uncertainty and household pressures.
"Instead of focusing on the people's struggles, they are busy staging political scripts ahead of the next general election," he added.
Mahfuz urged political parties to end what he termed "cosmetic politics" and act with clarity and integrity if cooperation had genuinely broken down, rather than prolonging internal disputes that could undermine public trust in the political system.
Mahfuz served as the Pokok Sena member of parliament for multiple terms.
Pakatan Rakyat governed Kedah after the 2008 general election until 2013, with Pas leading the state administration.
The coalition later collapsed in 2015 due to irreconcilable differences between Pas and DAP, particularly over ideological and policy disagreements, and was subsequently replaced by PH without Pas.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah youth among first to benefit from Perkeso's 24-hour protection
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A young man from Sabah who suffered a serious spinal injury after falling down a staircase at home has become among the first beneficiaries of the Non-Work-Related Accident Scheme, or Lindung 24/7 protection scheme, which came into effect on June 1.
In a statement today, the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) said Arnold Nathanael Lister, 24, qualified for coverage as an active contributor despite the accident occurring outside working hours.
Perkeso said the assistance approved included full funding for his spinal implant surgery and treatment, as well as Temporary Disablement Benefit to replace lost income while he is medically unfit to work.
"The victim, from Penampang, is also receiving rehabilitation equipment, including a specialised wheelchair and a urinary drainage bag management device, along with free physiotherapy under our Return To Work (RTW) programme, subject to his readiness," it said.
Perkeso said the case highlighted the role of the scheme in protecting workers injured outside the scope of their employment.
It said that domestic accident victims previously had limited access to financial protection, but the new scheme now covered treatment costs and safeguards contributors' livelihoods.
On June 6, Arnold slipped while using a staircase to go to the bathroom during renovation works at his home.
He suffered a fractured and compressed spine and is receiving treatment at Queen Elizabeth Hospital in Kota Kinabalu.
Perkeso also reminded employers and workers to ensure contributions remained up to date so that round-the-clock protection was available in the event of unforeseen accidents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor Customs foils RM8.5mil transnational cigarette smuggling bid at Tanjung Pelepas port
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Customs enforcement officers foiled a transnational cigarette smuggling attempt from Vietnam to the United Kingdom, seizing more than RM8.5 million worth of illicit cigarettes at the Tanjung Pelepas Port last month.
The 9.32 million sticks of cigarettes, valued at RM2.14 million with unpaid duties and taxes estimated at RM6.36 million, were discovered during a warehouse inspection within the port free zone on May 18.
The seizure is the largest of its kind in Johor this year.
Johor Customs director Aminul Izmeer Mohd Sohaimi said the 12.19-metre container was inspected at the warehouse at 2.30 pm.
The operation was conducted by the Gelang Patah enforcement branch Container Control Unit based on intelligence from the Southern Zone Intelligence Branch.
The inspection, carried out in the presence of a shipping company representative, revealed various brands of suspected untaxed cigarettes.
Based on initial checks, the container was declared under the Customs manifest transhipment form as carrying base paper reels.
"However, none of the declared goods was inside," Aminul Izmeer said at a press conference at Menara Kastam here today.
He said the container was believed to be in transit from Vietnam to the UK.
The seized cigarettes are valued at RM2,143,600, with duties and taxes estimated at RM6,365,560, bringing the total value to RM8,509,160.
Aminul Izmeer said the syndicate's modus operandi involved making false declarations to smuggle the cigarettes, which investigations showed were not meant for the local market.
Efforts to track down the syndicate and the warehouse owner are underway to assist in investigations under Section 133(1)(a) of the Customs Act 1967 for making a false declaration.
Cigarettes are a prohibited import item under Item 1, Third Schedule, Part II of the Customs (Prohibition on Imports) Order 2023.
Importation requires an import licence and tax stamps approved by the Customs director-general under Regulation 42(1) of the Customs Regulations 2019.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negeri Sembilan DAP to keep over half of its incumbents for state polls
Article:
SEREMBAN, June 10 — Negeri Sembilan DAP will retain more than half of its incumbent representatives as candidates in the upcoming state election as it seeks to maintain its impressive record of winning every seat it has contested since 2013.
DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said the party’s candidate list is now in its final stages and an announcement will be made within the next few days.
He said the party also intends to field several new faces in the election as it seeks to defend the 11 seats it won in the last state election.
“The candidate list will be announced early to give them time to make preparations and familiarise themselves with the constituencies they will contest, particularly the new candidates.
“Any elected representative who is replaced must assist and support the new candidate to ensure victory and maintain the party’s strength,” he told reporters after launching the Lobak Electronic Screen at Dataran Lobak here today.
Previously, Loke, who is also Negeri Sembilan DAP chairman, said that he would defend the Chennah seat in the upcoming election.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced the dissolution of the Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly effective June 5 to pave the way for the state election, which must be held within 60 days.
The 2023 Negeri Sembilan state election saw Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) form the state government after securing 31 of the 36 seats.
PH won 17 seats while BN secured 14 seats, with Perikatan Nasional winning five seats.
Negeri Sembilan became the second state to dissolve its legislative assembly after Johor did so on June 1.
Loke said Johor DAP has yet to submit its full list of candidates for finalisation. — Bernama

Title: Negeri Sembilan Bersatu says it will contest election under PN banner despite PAS split
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Negeri Sembilan Bersatu has said it will continue to contest under the Perikatan Nasional (PN) banner in the next state election, despite PAS’ decision to end political cooperation with the party.
According to
Utusan Malaysia
, Negeri Sembilan Bersatu chief Mohamad Hanifah Abu Baker said Bersatu’s victories in the previous state election were secured on a PN ticket, meaning voters had given their mandate to the coalition.
“Their support should be given attention by PN and be considered by the coalition as it prepares to face the coming state election.
“At the same time, PAS’ decision is rather confusing because it is said to have severed political ties with Bersatu but remains together in PN, which we are also part of,” he was quoted as saying.
PN currently holds five seats in the Negeri Sembilan state assembly, three through PAS and two through Bersatu.
Meanwhile,
Utusan Malaysia
reported that Negeri Sembilan PAS commissioner Mohd Fairuz Mohd Isa reportedly said no decision had been made on whether PAS would use the PN logo or contest under the PN ticket in the next state election.
He reportedly said matters concerning the state election and the use of logos would be discussed later by PAS’ central leadership, with the state chapter to abide by the decision made at the top level.
The remarks came as PAS central committee member and PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said earlier today that PAS would discuss PN’s position with Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP) this week, following its decision to end political cooperation with Bersatu.

Title: Malaysian banks honour 68 staff who helped save RM139m from scammers last year
Article:
PETALING JAYA, June 10 — A total of 68 outstanding employees were honoured today for what banks described as their “exceptional vigilance and quick actions”, which helped prevent over RM139 million from being stolen by financial fraudsters in 2025.
The banking personnel were feted at the second consecutive #JanganKenaScam Bank Heroes Recognition Event 2026, a nationwide anti-scam initiative organised by The Association of Banks in Malaysia (ABM) and The Association of Islamic Banking and Financial Institutions Malaysia (Aibim), in close collaboration with the police.
According to data released at the event, the ground-level vigilance of these employees complemented wider structural security measures, resulting in a staggering RM1.19 billion in fraudulent and suspicious transactions being blocked across the industry throughout last year.
This figure represents a three-fold increase year-on-year compared to 2024. The associations said the numbers reflect the aggressive scale of modern financial crime and the corresponding ramp-up in banking defences.
National Taskforce to Combat Fraud chairman Syed Ahmad Taufik Albar commended the dedication of the front-line staff, describing them as the crucial final line of defence against increasingly sophisticated syndicates.
“As personnel on the ground against financial crime, their swift actions and commitment have been instrumental in detecting suspicious activities and preventing fraudulent transactions before further losses can occur,” Syed Ahmad Taufik, who is also the group chief executive officer of Community Financial Services at Maybank, said in a statement.
National Taskforce to Combat Fraud chairman Syed Ahmad Taufik Albar speaks at the #JanganKenaScam Bank Heroes Recognition Event 2026 in Petaling Jaya. — Picture courtesy of ABM
The industry’s collective efforts yielded a 46 per cent increase in the total volume of successfully blocked fraudulent transaction attempts in 2025 compared to the previous year, he added.
#JanganKenaScam in third year
The #JanganKenaScam campaign, originally launched in 2023, acts as a primary vehicle for nationwide scam awareness, public education, and strategic knowledge sharing between commercial banks and law enforcement agencies.
Organisers said the annual recognition event underscores the critical partnership between corporate banking institutions and the authorities.
Frontline personnel such as tellers, customer service representatives, and branch managers are the critical first security layer between scammers and customers since they are trained to spot signs of fraud attempts.
Also present at the ceremony was Commissioner of Police Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa, the head of Bukit Aman’s commercial crime investigation department. He said combating commercial crimes and scams requires continued strong collaboration and support from the banking industry.
“Through close cooperation and information sharing, banks and law enforcement agencies have strengthened efforts to better protect the public against evolving scam threats,” he said.
Financial scams have become more sophisticated over the years, prompting Bukit Aman to issue severe warnings regarding a surge in AI-driven financial and investment scams across Malaysia.
It said scammers are now heavily utilising AI-generated deepfake videos and fake AI-powered investment apps to defraud victims of millions of ringgit, among others.
Banks pledge tougher security framework
Syed Ahmad said the banking industry is continuously strengthening technical security architectures, but stressed the importance of boosting public literacy on financial scams to neutralise threats before they reach the execution stage.
Malaysians lost a total of RM1.57 billion to online and financial scams in 2024, according to police and Home Ministry data, a steep 84 per cent increase year-on-year.
A total of 35,368 cybercrime cases involving financial scams were officially reported during that year alone.

Title: JB Sentral closure: KTMB activates bus transfers amid RTS Link project works from June 11
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Train services at JB Sentral Station will be temporarily suspended starting tomorrow to facilitate the launching of the truss structure for the Johor Bahru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link Project.
Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) in a statement today said that during the closure period, passengers involved in the ETS EP9535 and EG9449 trains will continue their journey using the intermediary bus service provided between Kempas Baru Station and JB Sentral.
Designated areas for passenger pick-up and drop-off will also be provided at both locations, with staff and ambassadors stationed at both stations to assist passengers.
“Passengers are advised to adhere to the signs and instructions from the staff to ensure safe and smooth movement during the closure period,” according to the statement.
Affected passengers can contact the KTMB Call Centre at 03-9779 1200 for further information. — Bernama

Title: PAS taps Annuar Musa to lead Johor election campaign
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 10 — PAS has appointed former federal minister Tan Sri Annuar Musa as its Johor election director ahead of the upcoming state polls.
Johor PAS information chief Mohd Firdaus Jaffar said the appointment was aimed at strengthening the party’s preparations for the election.
“PAS Johor welcomes Tan Sri Annuar Musa’s appointment as the party’s state election director.
“We are confident that his experience and leadership will help strengthen the preparations as well as coordination for the Johor polls,” he said in a statement today.
Mohd Firdaus said the party machinery in Johor has been instructed to remain united and fully focused on election preparations.
“With the election approaching, the entire Johor PAS machinery is called to continue strengthening its unity within the ranks and fully focus on the election preparations,” he said.
Annuar, a former Umno leader and federal minister, joined PAS in 2023.
The 70-year-old has since played an active role in PAS and Perikatan Nasional (PN), particularly on political strategy.
Mohd Firdaus also said Johor PAS supports the party leadership’s decision to end political cooperation with Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu).
“Obviously, the issue was explained and discussed during the special meeting held at the Johor PAS office last night.
“All members had unanimously expressed their commitment to respect, comply with and implement the decision,” he said.
Mohd Firdaus said Johor PAS does not believe the move will affect efforts to unite the ummah, which remains a core objective of the party.
He said the state chapter would continue to support any political understanding or electoral pact decided by the party’s central leadership.
Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution of the state assembly on June 1 after obtaining the consent of Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail.
The dissolution paves the way for a state election to be held within 60 days.

Title: Sabah baker injured in home accident becomes early beneficiary of Socso’s 24-hour protection scheme
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — A young man from Sabah who fell from a staircase at home has become one of the first beneficiaries under the Non-Work-Related Accident Scheme (SKBBK), or LINDUNG 24 Jam, which took effect on June 1.
In a statement today, the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) said the victim, Arnold Nathanael Lister, 24, qualified for coverage as an active contributor, despite the accident happening outside working hours.
Perkeso added that the approved aid includes full funding for his spinal implant surgery and treatment, plus Temporary Disablement Benefit to replace lost income while he is medically unfit to work.
“The victim, from Penampang, Sabah, is also receiving rehabilitation equipment, including a specialised wheelchair and a urinary drainage bag management device, along with free physiotherapy under our Return To Work (RTW) programme, based on his readiness,” the statement read.
The organisation said the case reflects LINDUNG 24 Jam’s role in protecting workers injured outside the scope of their employment.
Perkeso noted that previously, domestic accident victims often struggled to get financial protection, but the new scheme now covers treatment costs and safeguards the livelihoods of contributors.
Arnold Nathanael Lister, 24, a baker from Kota Kinabalu, slipped on June 6 while using a staircase to go to the bathroom, as his home in Penampang was being renovated.
He suffered a fractured and compressed spine and is now being treated at Queen Elizabeth Hospital, Kota Kinabalu.
Perkeso reminded employers and workers to keep contributions up to date so that 24-hour protection is in place for any unforeseen accident. — Bernama

Title: Five days, unlimited durian, zero apologies: Penang’s new Hong Kong tour is every fruit lover’s dream
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, June 10 — Penang has rolled out its first official durian-themed tourism package in Hong Kong, marking a major step forward in the state’s bid to boost its international tourism profile and promote its signature durian experiences.
State Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said the initiative, a collaboration between Tourism Malaysia’s Hong Kong office, Penang Global Tourism (PGT) and Wing On Travel, aims to draw more Hong Kong tourists through a travel package built entirely around Penang’s celebrated durian industry.
“Hong Kong remains one of Penang’s key international visitor markets, with travellers from the city known for their appreciation of culinary experiences, cultural heritage and immersive travel offerings. This collaboration represents a milestone for Penang tourism.
“For the first time, we are packaging one of Penang’s most distinctive seasonal attractions, which is our world-renowned durian, into a dedicated tourism experience specifically tailored for the Hong Kong market,” he said in a statement released during the launch in Hong Kong today.
Wong said Penang’s durian tourism sector has evolved beyond the consumption of the fruit into a distinctive experiential product that combines gastronomy, nature and local culture.
Visitors are increasingly seeking activities such as durian buffets and countryside experiences in Balik Pulau, the only place in Malaysia where durians are cultivated on an island.
